nameMeanings.name: Gower,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name for someone from Gohiere, the Old French form of what is now known as Goelle, the country north of Paris.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Anglo-Norman French Goier, Joier, denoting a man from any of several places in Normandy and Picardy called Gouy, Joué, or Jouy (derived from the Latin personal name Gaudius, Old French Goi, Joi + -acum ‘joy’).
English (of Norman origin): from a Norman personal name, Go(h)ier, ancient Germanic Godehar, cognate with the Old English name mentioned at Gooder .

variants.name: Gooder
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from a Middle English personal name Godere, Old English Gōdhere, composed of the elements gōd ‘good’ + here ‘army’.
English: alternatively, a variant of Goodair, a nickname from Middle English god(e), gu(o)d ‘good’ + heir(e), (h)air(e) ‘heir’.

variants.name: Gowers
meanings: English (Essex and Suffolk): variant of Gower , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Gurr
meanings: German: derisive nickname for a useless person or a wicked woman, from Middle High German gurre ‘bad mare’.
English (Kent and Middlesex): variant of   Gower   or   Gore  .

variants.name: Jowers
meanings: English (Essex and Suffolk): variant of Gower , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Goward
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Gower , with excrescent -d.

nameMeanings.name: Esq
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Durham and Yorkshire): variant of   Ash  .
History: This (see 1 above) is one of the variants of the name of a Swiss Mennonite family, originally spelled Oesch and later, in Germany, Esch .

variants.name: Esch
meanings: habitational name from any of several places called Esch, from Old High German asc ‘ash’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a prominent ash tree.

from Middle High German ezzisch ‘communally owned seeded field’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived by an area where grain was grown or perhaps a status name for someone who owned such land.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Gower, Esq was born about 1390, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Joan. He died from 11 July 1458 to 31 December 1458, in Clapham, Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Gower, Esq est né environ 1390, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Joan. Il est décédé du 11 juillet 1458 au 31 décembre 1458, à Clapham, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Gower, Esq wurde um 1390 in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Joan mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb von 11. Juli 1458 bis 31. Dezember 1458 in Clapham, Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Gower, Esq è nato il circa 1390 nel Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Joan. Morì da 11 luglio 1458 a 31 dicembre 1458, nel Clapham, Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Gower, Esq(은)는 대략 1390년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 태어났다. 그는 Joan(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1458년 7월 11일부터 1458년 12월 31일까지 영국 잉글랜드 수리 클라프아에서 사망했다.
